decisionMemorySummary.note: Aggregates Codex recommendation snapshots compared against final human workbench decisions. Missing historical Codex recommendations remain unavailable and are not inferred.

meta.note: Codex-first conservative review. Human QC still required before merge.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows oil-can-with-drip (engine oil warning). Machine top match q0611 indicates 'engine oil pressure too low', but source French options refer to 'pression d'essence'/'tuyau d'essence' (fuel) — option set does not contain the correct oil-related answer; localization/options conflict with image.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0611 is semantically appropriate for the image, but source options talk about fuel (essence) not oil; cannot map master correct option to any provided localized option.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Conflicting candidate signals: some items state alternating high/low beams at night on pedestrian crossing is allowed (q0318), others state it is not allowed at unlit intersections (q0061). Source phrasing ambiguous; cannot reliably pick Yes/No.

Source extracted answer = Yes (A) but top matcher q0642 labels the statement as Wrong (i.e., red car may NOT drive in this lane). Visual semantics ambiguous and candidate conflict—do not accept automatically.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source asks to remain behind slow traffic ('rester dans la file'). q0520's correct meaning is 'follow the vehicles in front' — meaning matches source option D.

Dashboard icon image ambiguous: OCR/gloss describes thermometer/wavy lines (temperature) while source asks about 'engine needs fuel'. Machine candidates conflict (oil pressure, seatbelt, fuel/oil low). Cannot confidently map to existing qid or answer.

Source asserts stopping before stop marking and observing at the shown traffic signal (red). No clear existing candidate matches this exact traffic-light + stop-line instruction; recommend new question with correct = Yes (A).

Image shows oil-can-with-drip (engine oil warning). Machine top match q0611 indicates 'engine oil pressure too low', but source French options refer to 'pression d'essence'/'tuyau d'essence' (fuel) — option set does not contain the correct oil-related answer; localization/options conflict with image.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0611 is semantically appropriate for the image, but source options talk about fuel (essence) not oil; cannot map master correct option to any provided localized option.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image = triangular yellow warning sign (intersection warning). q0710 corresponds to 'which kind of sign' with correct 'warning sign' → French 'panneau danger' (A). Top candidate q0764 is a different specific-intersection question, so approved q0710 instead.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0764 asks for specific intersection type, not the sign category; q0710 better matches 'kind of sign' question.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Conflicting candidate signals: some items state alternating high/low beams at night on pedestrian crossing is allowed (q0318), others state it is not allowed at unlit intersections (q0061). Source phrasing ambiguous; cannot reliably pick Yes/No.

Correct behaviour encountering children = slow down and yield. q0178 correct = 'reduce speed and go slowly or stop to yield' which corresponds to French option B ('ralentir et céder le passage'). Top candidate list favored q0105 (vehicle-cut-in) but q0178 better matches 'children' context.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0105 concerns another scenario (vehicle cutting in); q0178 semantically matches encountering children and aligns with the French options.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Candidate q0192 is related (uphill vs downhill yielding) but the French prompt wording is ambiguous about which vehicle is 'en bas' and who is descending. Image plus semantics uncertain — do not auto-approve.
Image shows a curve warning sign (no explicit speed-limit sign). Top candidate q0481 and others disagree on correct speed — option-set / image semantics mismatch. Needs human review or clearer image.

Question asks numeric safety distance for a warning triangle on a highway (entre 50 et 150 m). No clear existing qid in top candidates matches this exact regulatory-distance item — create new. Local answer B matches extracted correct.

Source extracted answer = Yes (A) but top matcher q0642 labels the statement as Wrong (i.e., red car may NOT drive in this lane). Visual semantics ambiguous and candidate conflict—do not accept automatically.

Question asks which light to use when moving to a slower (deceleration) lane — correct action is right-turn indicator. q0391 has correctOption = 'turn on the right-turn signal' which maps to localized option B.

Image shows a circled continuous white line but candidate q0833 (double broken lines = slowdown/yield) is similar yet option set does not include 'slowdown/yield'. None of the provided French options clearly map to the top candidate; visual/option mismatch — needs human review.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top candidate q0833's meaning (slowdown/yield) does not match any of the source options; option-set / image semantics mismatch.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Top candidate q0350 ('destroyed' license) is close but source says 'damaged' — legal nuance could differ. Not enough confidence to approve existing qid; needs human/legal check.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top candidate q0350 uses 'destroyed' vs source 'damaged' — potential legal difference, so top match not accepted automatically.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Legal/administrative claim (suspension of Carte Grise for missing insurance sticker) is jurisdiction-specific and top candidates are not semantically aligned; require subject-matter review.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: No top candidate with clear matching legal semantics; cannot approve automatically.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement about placing triangle 'less than 50 m behind' conflicts with candidate items (some indicate within/at least 50 m). Ambiguous/legal detail; keep unresolved for human review.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top candidates disagree about the 50 m direction (within vs at least); cannot select automatically.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Which document authorizes police to immobilize vehicle — candidate matches are weak and legal practice varies (ID vs Carte Grise). Insufficient signal to approve; requires human/legal confirmation.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top candidates are semantically distant and do not clearly map to the source options; cannot accept automatically.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source ROW statement equals q0080 prompt (switch to high beam on dark roads; following vehicle must not use high beam). French options Yes/No -> correct = Yes (A).
Source asks to remain behind slow traffic ('rester dans la file'). q0520's correct meaning is 'follow the vehicles in front' — meaning matches source option D.
Statement denies changing lanes without signalling even if no vehicle is behind. q0073 correctRow = Wrong (i.e. 'No') — matches source (B No).
Source: police may suspend licence if you lend car to someone whose licence was revoked. q0390 correctOption C matches that meaning.
Source asks which vehicle ahead must not be overtaken; option A = 'police car on duty'. q0386 correct = police car on duty -> match.

Dashboard icon image ambiguous: OCR/gloss describes thermometer/wavy lines (temperature) while source asks about 'engine needs fuel'. Machine candidates conflict (oil pressure, seatbelt, fuel/oil low). Cannot confidently map to existing qid or answer.
Image + prompt ask allowed movements; q0746 correct = 'go straight or turn right'. Source option D = 'aller tout droit ou tourner à droite' corresponds to that meaning.
Source: at intersection without crosswalk with pedestrians, must slow/stop to yield. q0137 correct = reduce speed or stop to yield -> matches source option A.

Source asserts stopping before stop marking and observing at the shown traffic signal (red). No clear existing candidate matches this exact traffic-light + stop-line instruction; recommend new question with correct = Yes (A).
Sign is triangular yellow with wheelchair — means 'watch for disabled people'. q0749 meaning 'watch for disabled people' matches source option A 'attention aux handicapés'.
Source ROW statement matches master q0016 (observe signs/markings, slow down, refrain from honking). Source Yes = Right.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source asks if U-turn allowed provided it doesn't disturb traffic; master q0240 has same wording and correctRow Right. Source Yes maps to Right.
Statement about skidding on icy roads and using engine braking matches q0102 (correctRow Right). Source Yes corresponds to Right.
Symbol is front windshield wiper; master q0603 correct meaning 'windshield wiper' — source option A ('l'essui-glace du pare-brise avant') matches that meaning.

Blue circular bicycle sign indicates bicycle/non-motorized vehicle lane/circulation; master q0845 meaning 'bicycle lane' corresponds to source option D ('circulation pour les véhicules non motorisés').

Traffic-light prompt maps to master q0807 ('intersection warning'). Source option A ('le conducteur approche une intersection') matches that meaning; mapping is plausible though candidate scores vary.
Image shows red light at intersection. Master q0194 states you may NOT speed up to pass (correctRow Wrong). Source No = B matches Wrong.
Statement that probationary-license drivers may not tow a trailer matches q0342 (correctRow Right). Source Yes = A.
Sign shows two opposite arrows in warning triangle = two-way traffic. Master q0656 meaning 'two-way traffic' maps to source option A ('circulation dans les deux sens').
Source statement = 'vehicles may stop/drive on emergency strip/shoulder after accident' is false. Top machine match q0005 has same ROW prompt and correctRow 'Wrong' → French answer 'No' (B).
Statement that drunk driving incurs ≥3 years imprisonment is presented as false by machine q0353 (correctRow 'Wrong'); maps to French 'No' (B).
Matches q0004: when changing lane driver should signal in advance, observe and keep safe distance — statement is true → French 'Yes' (A).

Image = triangular yellow warning sign (intersection warning). q0710 corresponds to 'which kind of sign' with correct 'warning sign' → French 'panneau danger' (A). Top candidate q0764 is a different specific-intersection question, so approved q0710 instead.
items.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0764 asks for specific intersection type, not the sign category; q0710 better matches 'kind of sign' question.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source asks how to drive when leaving highway — correct action is enter the deceleration/slower lane. q0469 correct = 'enter the deceleration lane'; French option with that meaning is B ('entrer dans une voie moins rapide').
Road marking shows curved arrow with X → indicates U-turn prohibited. q0805 correctOptionText = 'no U-turn'. French option with that meaning is B ('interdit de faire demi-tour').
Image shows ETC/toll sign. q0772 correct = 'toll station with ETC'; French option meaning 'télépéage' is D, so map master correct to French D.
Visual and machine top match q0255: 'Cannot overtake in this situation' is true. French 'Yes' (A) matches correct response.

Conflicting candidate signals: some items state alternating high/low beams at night on pedestrian crossing is allowed (q0318), others state it is not allowed at unlit intersections (q0061). Source phrasing ambiguous; cannot reliably pick Yes/No.

Correct behaviour encountering children = slow down and yield. q0178 correct = 'reduce speed and go slowly or stop to yield' which corresponds to French option B ('ralentir et céder le passage'). Top candidate list favored q0105 (vehicle-cut-in) but q0178 better matches 'children' context.
items.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0105 concerns another scenario (vehicle cutting in); q0178 semantically matches encountering children and aligns with the French options.

Text-statement matches q0566 (seat belts reduce injuries). ROW True corresponds to French option A (Yes). Machine top match supports semantic match.
Statement 'overtaking prohibited on downhill mountain road' semantically matches q0086 (Right/True). French option A = Yes/True.
